sourceMovie.title: Executive Decision
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Executive Decision (1996) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Stuart Baird's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Dark Knight Rises
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Executive Decision clicked for you, The Dark Knight Rises (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Executive Decision clicked for you, Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Executive Decision?
faq.answer: Try The Dark Knight Rises, Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol, United 93—each shares DNA with Executive Decision while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Executive Decision?
faq.answer: Executive Decision (1996) is best described as Action, Adventure, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Executive Decision worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
